frameworks.name: predictive coding
frameworks.one_line: Perception integrates feedforward sensory signals with internally generated feedback expectation signals.

frameworks.name: layer-specific fMRI
frameworks.one_line: Cortical-layer fMRI dissociates feedforward (middle) from feedback (deep/superficial) signals in V2.

core_question: Do high-confidence false percepts arise from top-down feedback signals or from spontaneous feedforward input activity in early visual cortex?
key_assumptions: Feedback signals target deep and superficial cortical layers
Feedforward sensory input targets middle (input) cortical layers
Auditory cues can implicitly induce orientation expectations
manipulations: Auditory cues signaling most likely upcoming orientation (expectation)
Trial-by-trial perceptual orientation discrimination with confidence rating
Comparison across V2 cortical layers (deep, middle, superficial)
key_findings: Expectations induced orientation-specific templates in deep and superficial layers of V2 without affecting perception
Orientation of high-confidence false percepts was reflected in middle input layers of V2
Prevalence of high-confidence false percepts correlated with everyday hallucination severity in a separate online sample
interpretations: False percepts may arise from a feedforward mechanism reflected by spontaneous stimulus-like activity in input layers, independent of top-down expectation signals
Abnormal perceptual experiences (hallucinations) may share this feedforward false-percept mechanism
